subject: masb-agent-research
verdict: Solid foundational agent with clear persona-capability alignment but multiple high-severity issues around missing headless mode, ceremony overhead, a broken reference, and activation behavior leaking from bootloader. One critical finding: CAPABILITIES template absent from assets/.
summary: masb-agent-research is a well-structured memory agent with strong persona coherence: it knows exactly who it is (expert en veille technique Android) and what it does (research-patterns, enrich-spec, verify-approach). The primary opportunities are reducing activation ceremony for power users, adding a headless/fast-lookup path, resolving the broken capability-authoring.md reference, and trimming bootloader content back into the sanctum. The persona was treated as investment and was never flagged as waste.

agent_profile.title: Expert en Veille Technique
agent_profile.mission: Trouver les patterns, APIs et bonnes pratiques officiels qui rendent chaque phase Android implémentable -- sans jamais inventer ce que la documentation ne dit pas.

capabilities.name: research-patterns
capabilities.note: Recherche patterns/APIs/bonnes pratiques pour une phase, produit findings.md

capabilities.name: enrich-spec
capabilities.note: Enrichit la spec avec recommandations sourcées via enrichment-proposal.md

capabilities.name: verify-approach
capabilities.note: Vérifie conformité d'une approche selon skills officiels, produit conformity-report.md

detailed_analysis.leanness: Lean overall; three curseur blocks and one role meta-explanation repeat principles already in CREED but none constitute whole-capability ceremony.
detailed_analysis.architecture: Solid memory agent topology and activation spine. One high finding: capability-authoring.md reference does not exist.
detailed_analysis.determinism: 2 determinism leaks found: structural merge rules in prompt (high), template placeholder scanning in prompt (low). No intelligence leaks.
detailed_analysis.customization: customize.toml well-structured for memory agent. One medium finding: secondary config mechanism in init-sanctum.py.
detailed_analysis.enhancement: Strong domain framing. High findings: no headless mode, no wake.py failure recovery, no fast-lookup path, over-ceremonied daily waking.
detailed_analysis.agent-cohesion: Persona and capabilities align well. Medium tension between CREED boundary and enrich-spec's pattern recommendations.
detailed_analysis.sanctum-architecture: Three Laws and Sacred Truth present. Critical: CAPABILITIES template missing from assets/. High: activation behavior leaking from bootloader.

sanctum.note: Sanctum is the built agent's runtime memory, distinct from the builder's .memlog.md

journeys.name: First Breath
journeys.steps: Scaffold sanctum via init-sanctum.py, discovery questions (working style, research preferences), identity naming, capability introduction, tool setup. Configuration-style with 5 domain questions + urgency detection.

journeys.name: Waking (expert)
journeys.steps: wake.py loads sanctum → become yourself → bind standing rules → Waking Mode with greeting. No fast path for returning experts.

journeys.name: Phase research
journeys.steps: Architecte activates phase → research-patterns consumes spec.md → produces findings.md → enrich-spec proposes enhancements → verify-approach checks compliance.

experience.headless: No headless mode available. All three capabilities require full conversational activation cycle.

themes.title: Missing headless and fast-lookup paths limit real-world utility
themes.root_cause: The agent was designed exclusively for the full MASB phase workflow with no shortcuts for returning experts or automators. All three capabilities require a phase context and produce phase-scoped files. Activation ceremony fires every session regardless of user expertise.
themes.action: Add a `quick-query` capability for inline sourced answers. Add `--headless` flag to wake.py. Introduce a stored preference in BOND.md for ceremony level.

themes.title: Bootloader carries sanctum-bound content
themes.root_cause: The SKILL.md On Activation section (lines 53-82) includes detailed mode-routing behavior, greeting scripts, and capability-style instructions that belong in sanctum templates per the bootloader-is-lean-by-design principle.
themes.action: Trim SKILL.md activation to the four-step routing skeleton only. Move greeting, pending-sparks handling, and capability-offer instructions into sanctum PERSONA or CAPABILITIES.

themes.title: Deterministic work delegated to LLM instead of scripts
themes.root_cause: Structural merge rules for config resolution and placeholder scanning in First Breath are described as prompt instructions when they are deterministic operations better served by scripts.
themes.action: Replace structural merge fallback in SKILL.md with a script call. Move placeholder scanning from First Breath into init-sanctum.py as a post-processing step.

themes.title: Capability prompts contain role boundaries already in CREED
themes.root_cause: The curseur blocks and role meta-explanations in research-patterns.md, enrich-spec.md, and verify-approach.md restate principles already encoded in CREED (Vérité sourcée, Humilité technique, boundaries). The persona already governs these behaviors.
themes.action: Trim redundant role carving from capability prompts. Keep only the artifact specification, the consumer, and the non-obvious nuance. Resolve the CREED boundary contradiction with enrich-spec.

strengths: Clear, well-defined persona (expert en veille technique, documentation-oriented) — the identity seed, CREED values, and communication style are coherent and specific to the research domain
Capabilities have clear artifacts, consumers, and quality gates (curseur pattern) — each one produces a distinct output consumed by a named downstream agent
First Breath uses config style with domain-specific discovery questions tailored to Android research preferences
Scripts are syntactically valid, wake.py correctly routes Waking vs First Breath mode, init-sanctum.py auto-discovers capabilities from frontmatter
customize.toml correctly identifies sanctum as primary customization surface for a memory agent
Sanctum templates include meaningful CREED values, standing orders, and dominion boundaries

recommendations.action: Create CAPABILITIES-template.md in assets/ to satisfy the standard 6-template contract and fix the critical sanctum architecture finding
Add a `quick-query` capability for inline Android pattern lookups without phase context, and add `--headless` support to wake.py
Trim SKILL.md On Activation to the four-step routing skeleton only; move greeting, pending-sparks, and capability-offer behavior into sanctum templates
Create references/capability-authoring.md or remove the dead reference from init-sanctum.py's generate_capabilities_md template
Replace the structural merge fallback prompt in SKILL.md with a script call (scripts/merge_config.py)
Document wake.py failure fallback analogous to the customization resolver fallback
Trim redundant curseur blocks and role meta-explanations from capability prompts; add seed examples to BOND-template.md

findings.title: CAPABILITIES template missing from assets
findings.evidence: All 6 standard templates must exist - INDEX, PERSONA, CREED, BOND, MEMORY, CAPABILITIES. CAPABILITIES-template.md is absent (auto-generated from frontmatter). TEMPLATE_FILES list omits it.
findings.recommendation: Add CAPABILITIES-template.md to assets/ or update to stub if auto-generation is the approved approach

findings.title: Referenced file references/capability-authoring.md does not exist
findings.evidence: init-sanctum.py embeds 'Load references/capability-authoring.md' into generated CAPABILITIES.md. The file does not exist in references/ or anywhere in the skill bundle.
findings.recommendation: Create references/capability-authoring.md or remove the dead reference from init-sanctum.py's generate_capabilities_md template

findings.title: Structural merge rules delegated to LLM fallback
findings.evidence: Prompt instructs LLM to manually apply structural merge rules across config files. Precise deterministic data-structure work.
findings.recommendation: Replace fallback with script call (uv run scripts/merge_config.py)

findings.title: No headless/automation mode
findings.evidence: All capabilities output files but activation always requires full persona cycle. wake.py has --pulse but no --headless flag.
findings.recommendation: Add --headless flag to wake.py that outputs identity silently and skips greeting ceremony

findings.title: No recovery path when wake.py fails
findings.evidence: SKILL.md mandates uv run scripts/wake.py with no fallback if uv unavailable or Python version missing.
findings.recommendation: Document manual fallback for wake.py failure analogous to customization resolver fallback

findings.title: No fast-lookup/quick-query capability
findings.evidence: All capabilities tied to phase workflow - require phase number N, spec file, produce output to masb-workspace/phases/N/.
findings.recommendation: Add fourth capability quick-query that returns inline sourced answer without phase context

findings.title: Daily waking ceremony over-applied
findings.evidence: Every session requires full ceremony - resolve customization, wake.py, become yourself, bind rules, execute Waking Mode with scripted greeting. No acceleration path for returning experts.
findings.recommendation: Introduce --fast flag or stored preference in BOND.md that skips greeting ceremony

findings.title: Activation behavior content leaking into bootloader
findings.evidence: On Activation section contains mode-routing behavior, greeting scripts, pending-sparks handling, capability instructions - belongs in sanctum.
findings.recommendation: Move mode-specific behavior into sanctum templates, keep only routing skeleton in bootloader

findings.title: Curseur ceremony in two capability prompts restates persona principles
findings.evidence: Both files end with Le curseur paragraph re-teaching honesty/sourcing principles already in CREED.
findings.recommendation: Replace with single terse line per file where non-obvious nuance exists, or remove entirely
findings.proposed_smallest: research-patterns.md: 'Marque comme hypothèse toute recommandation non sourcée depuis un skill officiel.' / verify-approach.md: remove - CREED already covers honesty.
findings.predicted_delta: None. CREED governs the same behaviors.

findings.title: Boundary contradiction between CREED and enrich-spec capability
findings.evidence: CREED says do not propose architecture but enrich-spec instructs agent to propose 'Patterns d'implémentation recommandés'.
findings.recommendation: Relax CREED boundary with carve-out for sourced technical recommendations, or narrow enrich-spec to catalog options only

findings.title: No handling for missing external skills
findings.evidence: research-patterns hardcodes context7-cli, android-cli, domain skills as sources but has no fallback when skills are not installed.
findings.recommendation: Add skill-provisioning detection as activation step or capability

findings.title: Secondary config mechanism reads config.yaml in init-sanctum.py
findings.evidence: init-sanctum.py reads _bmad/config.yaml for user_name/communication_language, creating parallel surface outside customize.toml.
findings.recommendation: Remove config.yaml codepath from init-sanctum.py; set defaults conversationally during First Breath

findings.title: First Breath too ceremonious for action-oriented user
findings.evidence: 5 discovery questions + identity + personality + capabilities + tools before research help. Urgency detection vague.
findings.recommendation: Define concrete urgency signals, add --minimal-setup flag

findings.title: Script path resolution ambiguous
findings.evidence: Script calls use bare relative paths (uv run scripts/wake.py) with no {skill-root}/ prefix.
findings.recommendation: Prefix all script references with {skill-root}/

findings.title: BOND template seeds are empty placeholders
findings.evidence: Domain sections exist but all seed content is {à découvrir}. Things to Remember and Things to Avoid are empty.
findings.recommendation: Add meaningful seed examples or default values to each BOND section

findings.title: Template placeholder scanning delegated to LLM
findings.evidence: Prompt instructs LLM to scan for remaining {placeholder} and replace.
findings.recommendation: Move placeholder scan into init-sanctum.py as post-processing step

findings.title: Parenthetical restates sourcing principle from CREED
findings.evidence: '(sourcés depuis les skills)' appended to section header. CREED already enshrines Vérité sourcée.
findings.recommendation: Drop the parenthetical

findings.title: Role meta-explanation redundant with CREED Boundaries
findings.evidence: Role explanation duplicates CREED Boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Drop role-explanatory sentence, keep consumer/artifact spec

findings.title: Curseur restates role boundary again
findings.evidence: Curseur repeats same role carve-out already at line 11.
findings.recommendation: Remove the curseur block

findings.title: Role preamble adds no dimension over CREED
findings.evidence: Two role-clarification sentences are only content beyond I/O spec. Define boundary already in CREED.
findings.recommendation: Cut both role clarifications. File reduces to 5 lines of pure I/O spec.
findings.proposed_smallest: Lis spec.md et findings.md. Propose dans enrichment-proposal.md des dépendances, contraintes techniques, patterns d'implémentation et points d'attention. L'Architecte valide et intègre.
findings.predicted_delta: Minimal. CREED Boundaries already prevent role encroachment.

findings.title: No proactive deprecated-pattern detection across phases
findings.evidence: verify-approach spots deprecation reactively but research-patterns has no proactive cross-reference step.
findings.recommendation: Add note to re-validate prior phase recommendations against current documentation

findings.title: No PEP 723 inline dependency block in init-sanctum.py
findings.evidence: Script lacks # /// script metadata block.
findings.recommendation: Add PEP 723 block with requires-python and dependencies

findings.title: No argparse in init-sanctum.py
findings.evidence: Script lacks --help self-documentation.
findings.recommendation: Add argparse with description and argument help text

findings.title: No json.dumps in init-sanctum.py
findings.evidence: Output may not be structured JSON.
findings.recommendation: Use json.dumps for structured output

findings.title: No argparse in wake.py
findings.evidence: Script lacks --help self-documentation.
findings.recommendation: Add argparse with description and argument help text

findings.title: No json.dumps in wake.py
findings.evidence: Output may not be structured JSON.
findings.recommendation: Use json.dumps for structured output

findings.title: No sys.exit() calls in wake.py
findings.evidence: May not return meaningful exit codes.
findings.recommendation: Return 0=success, 1=fail, 2=error via sys.exit()
